fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Alaska Speech-Language Pathologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Alaska - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: speech-language pathologist license reciprocity alaska, how to transfer speech-language pathologist license to alaska, speech-language pathologist compact alaska, alaska speech-language pathologist endorsement application

board.name: Board of Examiners in Audiology and Speech-Language Pathology
board.parent_dept: Alaska Department of Commerce, Community, and Economic Development

license_types.title: Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist
license_types.description: The full credential to independently practice speech-language pathology in Alaska. Requires a master's degree, clinical fellowship, and passing Praxis score.

license_types.title: Provisional SLP License
license_types.description: Issued to SLP graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship. Allows supervised practice in Alaska.

steps.title: Meet Education Requirements
steps.description: Possess a master’s degree or doctorate in speech-language pathology from a program accredited by the Council on Academic Accreditation in Audiology and Speech-Language Pathology.

steps.title: Complete Supervised Professional Experience
steps.description: Complete a supervised professional experience (SPE) that meets the requirements in the state's regulations.

steps.title: Pass the National Examination
steps.description: Achieve a passing score on the National Examination in Speech-Language Pathology.

documents.detail: Score of 162 or higher on test 5331.
CF supervisor attestation or ASHA CCC-SLP certificate.

telepractice_text: Alaska permits telepractice for licensed SLPs and is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact. The compact allows SLPs licensed in member states to practice across state lines. Telepractice is particularly important in Alaska given the state's rural and remote communities.
provisional_text: Alaska issues a Provisional SLP License for graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship, allowing supervised practice while fulfilling post-graduate experience requirements.
reciprocity_text: Alaska is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact. SLPs from other compact member states can practice in Alaska through a compact privilege. Traditional endorsement pathways are also available.

faqs.question: How much does an Alaska SLP license cost?
faqs.answer: The application and license fee is $250. Biennial renewal is also $250. Alaska has higher licensing fees compared to most other states.

faqs.question: Is Alaska part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?
Does Alaska allow telepractice for SLPs?
faqs.answer: Yes. Alaska permits telepractice and it is widely used due to the state's remote geography.

faqs.question: Does Alaska have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers speech-language pathologist practice in Alaska. To work in Alaska, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to Alaska?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 8 weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Alaska while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Alaska is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact. SLPs from other compact member states can practice in Alaska through a compact privilege. Traditional endorsement pathways are also available.

hero.title: Become a Speech-Language Pathologist in Alaska
hero.dek: Alaska Speech-Language Pathologist licenses require a master's degree, passing the national exam, and completion of a supervised professional experience.
